failure_surface: Excel formulas were saved with double equals signs (==) instead of single equals signs (=), making them invalid Excel formulas that cannot evaluate.
root_cause: When assigning formulas to openpyxl cells, the agent added an extra '=' prefix using f'={simple_formula}' while the formula variable already contained the leading '=' character, resulting in '==IF(...)' being written to cells instead of '=IF(...)'.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: When assigning Excel formulas to openpyxl cell values, ensure the formula string is not double-prefixed with '='; either define the formula without the leading '=' and add it during assignment, or assign the complete formula string directly without modification.
evidence: Verification output shows Row 2 cell value starts with '==IF' instead of '=IF'
Assignment used: ws[f'F{row}'] = f'={simple_formula}' which doubles the equals sign
All 15 rows (F2:F16) contain the same malformed formula pattern with double equals

memory_items.title: Formula Assignment Syntax in openpyxl
memory_items.lesson: When writing Excel formulas to openpyxl cells, do not add a separate '=' prefix if the formula string already contains the leading equals sign.
memory_items.generalization: Excel formulas assigned via openpyxl.cell.value must have exactly one leading '=' character; verify formula string format before assignment to avoid duplication.

memory_items.title: Formula Validation Before Saving
memory_items.lesson: After writing formulas to cells, always validate the actual cell contents match expected formula syntax before proceeding to save or mark task complete.
memory_items.generalization: Implement a validation step that reads back cell values after formula assignment to confirm correct formula structure rather than relying solely on successful write operation.

memory_items.title: Openpyxl Cell Value Conventions
memory_items.lesson: openpyxl requires formulas to be stored as strings starting with '=' in the cell's .value property; any additional characters before '=' will break formula evaluation.
memory_items.generalization: When programmatically constructing formulas, maintain awareness of how intermediate string manipulation affects the final formula syntax stored in spreadsheet cells.

edits.content:
### 4. Handling Formulas
- **Preservation:** When updating a single value, ensure referenced formulas are recalculated or remain valid.
- **Modification:** Never replace a formula string with a pre-calculated value unless explicitly instructed.
- **Syntax:** New formulas written via `openpyxl` must use standard Excel notation (e.g., `=A1+B1`).
- **Prefix Warning:** When assigning formula strings to cell `.value`, ensure only one leading '=' character exists. Do not concatenate additional '=' prefixes to formula strings that already contain the leading '='.
- **Circular Refs:** Avoid creating circular dependencies during logic implementation.
- **Validation:** After writing formulas, read back cell values to verify correct formula syntax (single '=' prefix) before saving.
